data_breach.note: Aggregated C1+C2 costs across all incident types involving data exfiltration

records_assumption_disclosure.model_anchor: IBM SA Cost of a Data Breach 2025
records_assumption_disclosure.rationale: The financial-impact model anchors the data-breach component on the IBM SA Cost of a Data Breach study, calibrated against industry-typical breach sizes (~25,000-100,000 records per incident). For context, the 'Other' industry heuristic of ~1 record per R50,000 of revenue implies this organisation likely holds approximately 200 sensitive records. The catastrophe modelling is reliable up to approximately 250,000 records for the 'Other' industry calibration. If the organisation actually holds more than 250,000 sensitive records under POPIA / GDPR / HIPAA / PCI DSS or other applicable regulation, the cat exposure in this report should be treated as a FLOOR estimate only. Common outliers: fintechs, health-tech aggregators, marketing platforms, data brokers, breach-list resellers - small-revenue entities with record holdings 10-1000x the industry average.

ransomware.note: Combined C4+C5 for backward compatibility; see scenarios_4cat for split

risk_probability._note: Reporting-only FAIR loss-event-frequency view. Three distinct annual-likelihood concepts with segregated bands. No scoring weight - new view of already-scored signals.

data_breach.label: Data-breach probability (annual)
data_breach.definition: Annual likelihood of a data breach - confidentiality loss / exfiltration of sensitive records. FAIR loss-event frequency p_breach = vulnerability x TEF x 0.30.
data_breach.band_anchor: Firm public breach-rate anchors: Cyentia IRIS (SMB material breach <2%/yr), BitSight, SecurityScorecard.

cyber_incident.label: Total cyber-incident probability (annual)
cyber_incident.definition: Annual likelihood of ANY modelled cyber incident - nested ABOVE the data-breach figure and always >= it. Independent union of the breach and ransomware channels: 1 - (1 - p_breach) * (1 - rsi_score x 0.30).
cyber_incident.band_anchor: Relative multi-channel posture bands (8/18/28). The combined breach+ransomware rate sits above per-org material-incident claims frequency (Coalition 2025 1.2%/yr <$25M to 5.7%/yr >$100M; Cyentia IRIS <2% SMB) because it aggregates both channels as an index; read as relative posture, not a calibrated annual claim rate. A frequency-calibration pass (ransomware LEF) is next.

availability_resilience.label: Availability resilience indicator (INDICATIVE)
availability_resilience.definition: Indicative resilience signal for outage / availability risk spanning DDoS and system / infrastructure-failure causes. Describes the RISK only - it is NOT a coverage statement (outage / system-failure cover varies by policy and over time). Base FAIR-anchored to outage data; per-control increments directional.
availability_resilience.basis: FAIR-anchored base (Uptime Institute AOA serious/severe outage rate ~3%/yr) plus availability-control increments: CDN/DDoS-mitigation (primary), hosting redundancy / multi-ASN (secondary), WAF (L7-flood only). Base FAIR-anchored; per-control increments directional.

regulatory_exposure.note: C2 = POPIA (Section 109; statutory ceiling R10M, 2% turnover used as internal capacity-scaling heuristic for s109(3) factors - not a statutory formula) + GDPR (4% uncapped) + PCI (R1M x non-compliance) + other jurisdictions (R2M each)

catastrophe_stack.note: Statutory maxima per applicable framework, scaled by the enterprise capacity factor for the entity's revenue band. Sanlam-scale (>= R10B) faces the full stack; a micro FSP (< R10M) faces 10% of the stack. Excludes uncapped POPIA Section 99 civil exposure - see Civil Liability Disclosure.

coverage_adjustment.basis: WAF / bot-manager blocked path-prober checkers. Catastrophe percentiles (P75-P99.6) widened to reflect unverifiable findings; median and mode anchored. Absence of a finding in affected checkers does not confirm absence of the underlying risk.

supply_chain_tail_adjustment.basis: Supply-chain effect flows through vulnerability uplift (see supply_chain_vulnerability_uplift) and the natural MC distribution shift. Separate cat-tail widening would double-count. Future work: conditional LGB widening anchored to MOVEit per-org Pareto distribution.

systemic_supply_chain_exposure.basis: Supply-chain severity is captured in the records-driven catastrophe C1 (a supplier-vectored breach still exposes the insured's record base) and the supply-chain signal raises p_breach via supply_chain_vulnerability_uplift (one signal, one channel). A correlated SYSTEMIC supply-chain catastrophe - many insureds compromised through a single shared vendor (MOVEit 2023 class) - is a portfolio accumulation risk disclosed here and managed at portfolio level, not modelled as an individual insured's loss (SA Covid-19 BI precedent: disclose correlated systemic loss rather than price it per policy).

return_periods.basis: severity-PML (single severe event); posture-independent; conditional on a severe breach, not an annual frequency

cover_ladder.basis: single-severe-event severity percentiles; cover-sizing tiers; posture-independent

loss_exposure.disclaimer: Figures presented are statistical model output and exclude civil liability (see Civil Liability Disclosure). Selection of cover limit is the responsibility of the insured in consultation with the broker. Phishield does not recommend a specific cover amount.

remediation_summary.catastrophe_note: The 1-in-250 catastrophe cover requirement is severity-driven and posture-independent - remediation lowers the LIKELIHOOD of loss, not the worst-case severity, so the cover figure is unchanged.
